description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular, stacked-stone wall forming the main body. The well is topped by a wooden frame consisting of two vertical posts and a horizontal beam, which supports a metal hand crank and wheel mechanism for drawing water. The stonework appears weathered and irregular, with visible moss or discoloration, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The wooden posts are rough-hewn and uneven, adding to the rustic appearance. The interior of the well is open, with no visible cover, allowing access to the water below. The overall construction is robust, with materials and design typical of wells found in parks, rural neighborhoods, or historical urban settings.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden frame and metal hand crank, featuring weathered stonework and an open top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular stone well, a central wooden post, and the metal hand crank mechanism directly facing the viewer.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full width of the wooden frame, both vertical posts, and the open stone well beneath the horizontal beam.
description_view_2: Rear view with the wooden post and hand crank mechanism centered, and the stone well visible behind the post.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the wooden frame, both posts, and the open stone well, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A metal hand crank and wheel mechanism is present, mounted on the wooden frame above the well.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a low, circular stone wall approximately 0.7 meters high.
